macro_summary: US equities closed Friday near record highs, with the S&P 500 at $720.65 (+0.11%) and the Nasdaq-100 up 0.94%. The session saw Trump announce the US would 'guide' ships through the Strait of Hormuz, easing oil supply fears — WTI settled at $101.81, down 3.1% from Thursday's spike. Semis led gains: SOXX surged 4.31% to $465.75, while the US10Y held at 4.38% after the Fed's April hold. Microsoft's Azure revenue jumped 40% (per Tech in Asia, Apr 29), partially offsetting OpenAI concentration concerns, while hyperscalers confirmed a record $630B 2026 capex plan (per Data Center Richness).
commodities_note: WTI eased to $101.81 (-3.1% from Thursday's $105.09 close) as Trump's naval escort pledge reduced immediate Hormuz closure risk, though analysts still cite a $160 tail-risk scenario. Gold retreated 0.74% to $4,610. The DXY was flat at 98.19. No data anomalies detected in benchmark feeds.

singapore.summary: The Singapore bank cluster remains in NIM compression day 47 with US10Y at 4.38% — still above the 4.20% reset threshold. OCBC (O39_SI) reports in 4 days, UOB (U11_SI) in 3 days — both face a binary that could resolve the compression thesis. DBS (D05_SI) extended to RSI 71 with a distorted 0.1:1 R:R, making it the least actionable name in the cluster.
singapore.thesis_status: Structurally impaired — awaiting earnings catalysts for NIM resolution
singapore.key_development: UOB's sizing R:R of 2.6:1 to structural support at $35.46 is the cluster's only workable risk/reward, but the Thursday earnings binary blocks entry. OCBC and DBS offer no actionable setups pre-print.

semiconductors.summary: SOXX surged to $465.75 (+4.31% Friday) but 7 of 8 semis names remain hard-blocked on extreme extension — only ASML holds a WATCH signal with workable R:R. MU (+7.4% Friday) and AMD (+2.6%) pushed further into overbought territory, while NVDA's pullback to $198.45 narrowed its extension slightly. The bifurcation is structural: mid-cap foundry (ASML) vs. mega-cap duration plays that have run too far.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Structurally bullish, tactically extended — ASML is the sole actionable name
semiconductors.key_development: TSMC's High-NA EUV delay (per ASML recent news) shifts the Standard EUV demand timeline forward, benefiting ASML's current order book. LITE reports Wednesday with all 4 priors averaging +9% upside — a bull-case band of $1,035 but no entry at +24.6% extension.

big_tech.summary: Microsoft's Azure +40% revenue jump (per Tech in Asia) and the OpenAI partnership restructure (per CNBC) offset the concentration-risk narrative, upgrading MSFT to WATCH. But AMZN and GOOG remain hard-blocked at +19% and +22% extensions respectively, with RSI readings above 78. PLTR reports tomorrow with a distorted R:R and 77x forward PE — the binary could swing the name ±9% but offers no pre-print entry.
big_tech.thesis_status: Mixed — Azure strength offsets OpenAI risk, but extreme extension blocks entry across the cluster
big_tech.key_development: The $630B hyperscaler capex plan for 2026 (per Data Center Richness) validates AI infrastructure demand but the market has already priced it in — AMZN's $200B allocation sent shares to RSI 78 with no pullback.

interconnected.name: Coherent
interconnected.reason: Received matching $2B NVDA photonics investment alongside LITE (per NVIDIA IR, Mar 2 2026) — the other leg of the AI optics supply-chain lock-in.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if LITE's May 6 earnings confirm AI optics revenue above $500M quarterly run-rate and COHR pulls back 8-10% from current levels.

interconnected.name: Arista Networks
interconnected.reason: Direct beneficiary of hyperscaler $630B capex — supplies the switches that connect GPU clusters in AI datacenters, with MSFT and META as top customers.
interconnected.entry_note: Revisit after AMZN and GOOG pull back to their SMA50 levels — ANET typically trades in sympathy with BigTech capex cycles.

interconnected.name: Marvell Technology
interconnected.reason: Custom ASIC competitor to AVGO and merchant silicon supplier for AWS Trainium — the hyperscaler custom-chip trend is a multi-year tailwind.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist on a 10-12% pullback from current levels; AVGO's PEG of 0.92 sets the cluster valuation floor.

interconnected.name: SK Hynix
interconnected.reason: MU's primary competitor in HBM memory — cross-read on memory pricing and hyperscaler demand when MU reports June 25.
interconnected.entry_note: Monitor MU's HBM bit-shipment guidance on June 25 earnings — SK Hynix typically moves in lockstep on memory cycle signals.

interconnected.name: TSMC (Taiwan-listed)
interconnected.reason: TSM's Taiwan-listed shares provide a cleaner pure-play foundry exposure without ADR premium/discount noise.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if TSM pulls back to $360-370 (SMA50 zone); CoWoS capacity sold out through 2026 is the structural anchor.

active_risks: Iran-Hormuz oil crisis enters week 9 with WTI at $101.81 — Trump pledges US naval escort through Strait of Hormuz, easing blockade fears but shipping has not normalised per IEA assessment.

US-China tech trade war escalates: Section 122 tariffs (15% surcharges), China rare-earth export controls, and COMAC aviation sanctions (CFM LEAP-1C engine export licenses revoked May 2025) constrain supply chains.
Federal Reserve held rates at 3.50-3.75% in April, citing Middle East inflation uncertainty — market pricing now favours a cut in late 2026 rather than mid-2026.
MAS maintained S$NEER appreciation pace at April 14 decision — Singapore banks face dual NIM compression from USD funding costs (US10Y 4.38%) and strong SGD until at least October 2026.
geopolitical.market_impact: Oil at $101.81 keeps energy premiums elevated but below the $109 panic spike — semis and BigTech have absorbed the logistics cost. Singapore banks (D05_SI, O39_SI, U11_SI) remain structurally compressed with US10Y above 4.20% reset threshold. ASML benefits from the supply-chain reshoring narrative as Western foundry capacity expands.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: Trump announced the US will 'guide' ships through the Strait of Hormuz (per Investor's Business Daily, May 4), signalling a shift from blockade risk to managed transit — WTI dropped $3.28 in response.
geopolitical.portfolio_action: No new entries into extended semis or BigTech — wait for pullbacks to SMA50 zones on ASML ($1,380-1,399), NVDA ($185-190), and TSM ($360-370). Singapore banks are on hold until earnings this week resolve the NIM trajectory.
